feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day, plus Polish public-holiday reminders

entry.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
entry.content: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

entry.title: Russia launches deadly, massive ballistic missile attack in Kyiv, Ukraine
entry.content: One person was killed and 16 others wounded in the overnight Russian attack on Kyiv, local authorities said.

entry.title: A look back at the 2022 FIFA World Cup final
entry.content: As the 2026 World Cup draws to an end, take a look back at the memorable 2022 final match between Argentina and France.

entry.title: How do Spain and Argentina stack up? World Cup final teams by the numbers.
entry.content: Forty-eight nations​ competed in the largest FIFA World Cup in history and two are left standing, with Spain in search of its second World Cup title and Argentina shooting for back-to-back​ championships.

entry.title: Restoring Britain’s health to 2014 levels could add 2% to GDP, thinktank says
entry.content: Health Foundation paper argues health is an economic asset and improving it could generate £72bn for public financesRestoring the deteriorating health of the UK’s population to the level of 2014 would boost GDP by 2% and generate a £72bn dividend for the public finances, research suggests.A paper by the Health Foundation thinktank, published on Sunday, argues the nation’s health should be valued by policymakers as an economic asset. Continue reading...

entry.title: How to watch the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup today
entry.content: Spain will face Argentina in the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup final match on July 19. Here's how and when to watch the soccer game.

entry.title: Spain and Argentina's World Cup trophy count before 2026 final
entry.content: When Spain and Argentina face off Sunday in the World Cup final, it will mark just the second meeting between the two sides in the tournament's history, and their first in 60 years.

entry.title: Messi vs. Ronaldo GOAT scorecard: The ultimate year-by-year comparison
entry.content: Lionel Messi will play in his third World Cup final on Sunday, a match that feels like the definitive end of an era for soccer's great 21st-century individual battle.

entry.title: Who is singing the national anthems at the 2026 World Cup final? Here are the performers at MetLife Stadium
entry.content: Here's who's been announced to perform the national anthems before Spain faces Argentina.

entry.title: Who is Spain's goalkeeper? Meet Unai Simon, the stone wall with most shutouts in a World Cup
entry.content: Unai Simon has kept six clean sheets at this World Cup, the most by any goalkeeper in a single tournament, and has conceded just one goal across seven matches.

entry.title: How long is halftime at the World Cup final? Projected show length and more to know in 2026
entry.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final will feature the tournament's first-ever halftime show, making the break much longer than a standard soccer match.

entry.title: Spain vs. Argentina projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 final at MetLife Stadium
entry.content: Forty-eight teams entered the 2026 FIFA World Cup, but only two remain alive for the title as Spain and Argentina meet at MetLife Stadium on Sunday.

entry.title: World Cup final pacific time kickoff: Spain vs. Argentina start time, schedule, channel, streaming to watch 2026 game live
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about the World Cup final matchup between Spain and Argentina, including live streaming information, TV schedules, and kickoff details.

entry.title: Argentina roster projection without Lionel Messi: What 2030 World Cup could look like if soccer legend retires
entry.content: Here's what the 2030 roster for La Albiceleste could look like without Messi at the helm.

entry.title: World Cup final referee 2026: Who are match officials and VAR for Spain vs. Argentina?
entry.content: World Cup final referee 2026: Who are match officials and VAR for Spain vs. Argentina?

entry.title: Police say husband asked spellcasters to hex his now missing wife
entry.content: Shortly before Maya "May" Millete vanished, authorities say her husband Larry messaged a spellcaster, "Please punish May and incapacitate her enough so she can't leave the house."

entry.title: Timeline: The disappearance of Maya Millete
entry.content: Maya Millete, 39, went missing in January 2021. What was happening between her and her husband before she disappeared and what does spellcasting have to do with it?

entry.title: California man charged in missing wife's murder left a review for spellcaster
entry.content: Investigators say Larry Millete contacted spellcasters to try to put a hex on his wife Maya so she wouldn't leave him.

entry.title: US strikes Iran's Revolutionary Guard over attack that killed troops in Jordan
entry.content: The U.S. military said Sunday it had ended its latest round of airstrikes on Iran, which included targeting Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard over an attack that killed American troops in Jordan

entry.title: Trump celebrates the World Cup as a US victory as he prepares to present trophy to the winning team
entry.content: President Donald Trump is set to present the World Cup trophy to the winner of Sunday’s final between Argentina and Spain

entry.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
entry.content: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independentVoters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials.According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

entry.title: US strikes Iran's Revolutionary Guard over an attack that killed troops in Jordan
entry.content: US strikes Iran's Revolutionary Guard over an attack that killed troops in Jordan

entry.title: 2 U.S. service members killed, 1 missing following Iranian strike on Jordan
entry.content: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran Saturday evening at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. service members.

entry.title: Colorado ICE center employee arrested after allegedly shooting protester
entry.content: An employee of a group that operates an ICE detainment center in Aurora, Colorado, was arrested after police said he shot and wounded a woman who was taking part in a protest.

entry.title: Taylor Farms recalls lettuce shipped to 27 states over cyclospora risk
entry.content: Taylor Farms has expanded a voluntary recall of its iceberg lettuce products sourced from central Mexico because of a potential link to the multistate cyclospora outbreak that has sickened people across the U.S. The California-based company said produc...

entry.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in U.S.
entry.content: A famous Cuban dissident artist, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country

entry.title: Healthcare costs associated with aging out of reach for many Americans
entry.content: One New York couple depleted their retirement and savings accounts, paying out-of-pocket for healthcare, before they were able to access Medicaid funds.

entry.title: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
entry.content: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested by federal authorities Saturday in Miami.

entry.title: The Justice Department Is Pulling Back on Prosecuting Corporate Crime
entry.content: Prosecutors have declined to charge businesses in a number of recent cases—despite believing that senior employees were involved in wrongdoing.

entry.title: Fans flock to see much-anticipated epic "The Odyssey"
entry.content: For the first time in what seems like forever, there has been a rush to see a new blockbuster on a really big screen, as Christopher Nolan's epic "The Odyssey" arrived in theaters this weekend. Noel Brennan has more.

entry.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
entry.content: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

entry.title: Tate brothers arrested by U.S. authorities, face additional rape charges in the U.K.
entry.content: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were taken into custody by federal law enforcement officials in Miami on Saturday, the U.S.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Big journeys begin with a single step.
Lucky Numbers: 12, 72, 30, 40, 69, 72

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Finish your workout in the morning – it's less likely to get done in the evening.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: 14% of Americans have skinny-dipped with a member of the opposite sex at least once.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Albus Dumbledore
entry.content: “The truth... It is a beautiful and terrible thing, and should therefore be treated with great caution.” — Albus Dumbledore

entry.title: Stage is set for high-stakes showdown at World Cup final
entry.content: The stage is set for Sunday’s high-stakes showdown at the World Cup final. With Spain facing off against Argentina, the game will also include a halftime show rivaling the Super Bowl’s, with big stars like Madonna and Justin Bieber. NBC News’ Jesse Kirsch reports.

entry.title: Dangerous storms put more than 100 million at risk across the Northeast
entry.content: Dangerous storms put more than 100 million people at risk across the Northeast on Saturday, with those storms already triggering flash flooding. It comes as Canadian wildfire smoke continues to linger over several American cities. NBC News’ Valerie Castro reports.

entry.title: FIFA World Cup schedule 2026: Complete match dates, times, team fixtures for soccer tournament
entry.content: The World Cup is going big for the 2026 edition of soccer's most illustrious tournament, expanding to 48 teams for the first time ever in the joint-hosted tournament held in USA, Canada, and Mexico.

entry.title: World Cup Golden Boot live rankings: Updated goal totals for Messi, Mbappe and more 2026 scoring leaders
entry.content: Here are the players at the top of this World Cup's chart so far as the leading scorers.

entry.title: The Titans’ next defensive superstar is hiding in plain sight
entry.content: Cedric Gray quietly finished among the NFL’s tackle leaders in 2025, but his biggest leap may still be ahead. With Robert Saleh and Gus Bradley building the Titans’ defense around his instincts and relentless pursuit, the third-year linebacker could emerge as one of the league’s breakout defensive stars in 2026.

entry.title: U.S. military says 2 service members killed in Jordan by Iranian attacks
entry.content: Two U.S. service members were killed Friday while defending against an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack in Jordan, and another service member is missing, U.S. Central Command said Saturday. NBC News’ Keir Simmons and Julie Tsirkin report.

entry.title: Most goals in World Cup history: Where Messi, Mbappe and more rank among FIFA's top scorers all time
entry.content: Here's a list of the players with their names on the scoresheet the most times in World Cup history.

entry.title: 49ers could have found a difference-maker in Upton Stout
entry.content: Upton Stout exceeded expectations as a rookie, showcasing the instincts, versatility and physicality that define elite nickel defenders. After finishing third on the 49ers in tackles, the second-year standout is poised to become a game-changing piece of San Francisco’s championship-caliber defense in 2026.

entry.title: Most goals in one World Cup: How Messi, Mbappe, Jude Bellingham compare to Just Fontaine's record
entry.content: Fontaine has held the record for most goals in a World Cup for 68 years.

entry.title: Why Connor Bedard signed 5-year contract for $75 million with Blackhawks, but not longer
entry.content: Chicago is keeping its star around for a while.

entry.title: Why Connor Bedard signed 5-year contract for $75 million with Blackhawks, but not longer
entry.content: Chicago is keeping its star around for a while.

entry.title: Kylian Mbappé scores gnarly 21st career World Cup goal, ties Lionel Messi record
entry.content: Kylian Mbappé is unbelievable. The France star scored his 21st career World Cup goal vs England in epic fashion.

entry.title: Kool-Aid McKinstry is about to silence every doubter
entry.content: Kool-Aid McKinstry flashed elite playmaking ability in 2025, but inconsistency kept him from reaching the next level. With Brandon Staley’s new defensive system in place, the young Saints cornerback could be on the verge of becoming one of the NFL’s premier shutdown defenders in 2026.

entry.title: Kool-Aid McKinstry is about to silence every doubter
entry.content: Kool-Aid McKinstry flashed elite playmaking ability in 2025, but inconsistency kept him from reaching the next level. With Brandon Staley’s new defensive system in place, the young Saints cornerback could be on the verge of becoming one of the NFL’s premier shutdown defenders in 2026.

entry.title: Arizona Cardinals training camp roster preview: LB Karson Sharar
entry.content: We will preview every player on the Cardinals roster leading up to training camp. This is about LB Karson Sharar.

entry.title: Kylian Mbappé ties Lionel Messi as World Cup's all-time leading scorer with 21st goal
entry.content: The goal also puts Mbappé one goal ahead of Messi in the 2026 Golden Boot race.

entry.title: Falkirk secure narrow win over youthful Genk
entry.content: An early goal from Leon McCann was enough to give Falkirk a narrow win over a Genk side who were playing their second pre-season friendly within 24 hours. Striker Ross MacIver fluffed his volley in front of goal, but full-back McCann was on hand at the back post to fire home. Noah Adedeji-Sternberg came close to an equaliser when the Belgium Under-21 winger was through on goal but fired against the face of the far post just after the hour mark.

entry.title: Messi faces a potential successor in Yamal as Argentina meets Spain in World Cup final
entry.content: After Lionel Messi had orchestrated another late, dramatic Argentina win, beating England to advance to the World Cup final, his teammate Enzo Fernández lifted Messi onto his shoulders, as the team danced around them.

entry.title: Pokémon Cards Beat the S&P 500 by 2.5x, But the Math Is a Lie
entry.content: Pokémon Cards Beat the S&P 500 by 2.5x, But the Math Is a Lie

entry.title: Netflix Stock's Last Decade Was Spectacular. But What Will the Next Decade Look Like?
entry.content: Netflix Stock's Last Decade Was Spectacular. But What Will the Next Decade Look Like?

entry.title: Cyclospora: iceberg lettuce recalled in 27 states and more products may follow
entry.content: Taylor Farms does not specify where products were served or sold, as US braces for weeks more of outbreakTaylor Farms recalled potentially contaminated shredded iceberg lettuce in 27 states on Friday, including lettuce distributed as recently as Thursday, as cases of cyclosporiasis continue rising in the US.The US is likely to see at least another two weeks of possible cases, since infections may have happened in recent days. And the expanding outbreak investigation could point to other products in coming days. Continue reading...

entry.title: Men's mile record broken for first time since 1999
entry.content: British runner Josh Kerr ran the mile in 3 minutes, 42.66 seconds on Saturday, breaking a record that has stood since 1999.

entry.title: Ben Nelson: Man United target tells Leicester he wants to leave
entry.content: A new central defender is not thought to be among Manchester United’s priority concerns this summer.In April, United handed a one-year deal to Harry Maguire, preventing the veteran Englishman from l...

entry.title: Why Graham Rahal, David Malukas cars didn't qualify in IndyCar Nashville race
entry.content: Graham Rahal and David Malukas were not able to run qualifying laps for the Nashville IndyCar race. Here are their starting positions for July 19.

entry.title: British Open 2026 tee times, pairings, featured groups for Sunday's Round 4 at Royal Birkdale
entry.content: Here's a look at the tee times for the final day of the 2026 British Open.

entry.title: Update On Chelsea And Their Pursuit Of This Crystal Palace Defender: What Should Alonso Do?
entry.content: In a recent report, The Times provided an update on Chelsea and their pursuit of Crystal Palace defender Maxence Lacroix. It has been stated that the Blues would have to pay £55m to land the French c...

entry.title: Argentina wear 1986 kit replica against England
entry.content: Argentina wear replica of the country's 1986 Fifa World Cup kit for the match against England at the Nations Championship.

entry.title: Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham benched for England’s third-place game
entry.content: Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham were named among the substitutes as England boss Thomas Tuchel raised eyebrows with his selection for the World Cup third-place play-off against France. The back-to-back European Championship finalists are playing for bronze in the Miami heat just three days on from the heartbreaking late semi-final collapse to Argentina in Atlanta.

entry.title: Northeast, Mid-Atlantic under tornadoes and flood risk as wildfire smoke lingers
entry.content: A severe weather threat is in effect from the Ohio Valley through the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast.

entry.title: How to watch England v France: TV channel and start time for World Cup third-place play-off
entry.content: England take on their rivals across The Channel in a battle to win the bronze match at the 2026 World Cup

entry.title: 2 U.S. service members killed in Jordan defending against Iranian attacks
entry.content: In addition to the two U.S. service members killed, another is currently missing in action, U.S. Central Command said on Saturday.

entry.title: Sandoval takes the bump for Saturday afternoon bout vs. Rays
entry.content: UPDATE We have a ballgame to play! OLDER And so the weather affects this one! Patrick Sandoval makes his Fenway Park debut for the Red Sox as the squad looks to keep this thing going! If…the weather decides to cooperate. Time: 4:10pm — Fenway Park, Boston, MA TV: NESN Radio: WEEI

entry.title: U.S. Forest Service employees taken hostage in California released safely
entry.content: Two U.S. Forest Service personnel were released safely from captivity Friday. A father and son are now in custody, accused of taking them hostage for hours.

entry.title: France doubles down on restricting access to Polymarket
entry.content: The country's gambling authority ordered ISPs to block access to the prediction market's website.

entry.title: Mexican president to attend World Cup final at Trump's invitation amid trade tensions
entry.content: It will be the first meeting between President Trump and Sheinbaum since December after months of verbal sparring. Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney is also expected to attend.

entry.title: Waymo says San Francisco service has resumed after one-hour pause
entry.content: This isn’t the first time power outages have caused issues for Waymo.

entry.title: Two U.S. Troops Killed in Iran Missile Attack on Jordan
entry.content: The casualties further escalated a weeklong exchange of fire where both sides have been striking increasingly sensitive targets.

entry.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
entry.content: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

entry.title: Two U.S. service members killed in Jordan and another is missing, military says
entry.content: Two U.S. service members were killed Friday while defending against an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack in Jordan, and another service member is missing, U.S.

entry.title: The Clock Is Now Ticking on Netanyahu’s Political Future
entry.content: Israel’s prime minister is bracing for a fight to stay in power as the parliament dissolves and the countdown to an election begins.

entry.title: Bills news: How is Keon Coleman preparing for pivotal season?
entry.content: Coming off a second straight up-and-down campaign, Keon Coleman says he is facing a “make or break” year with the Bills.

entry.title: “Went From GOAT to Fake Fighting”: Tom Brady’s Slap on Logan Paul Criticized by Patrick Mahomes’ Former Coach
entry.content: Tom Brady has the world talking after he slapped Logan Paul at Fanatics Fest in what is being built up to be a possible match between the duo in WWE. The sports entertainment giant’s SummerSlam pay-per-view is just around the corner, and the interaction was likely done to build interest in the event.

entry.title: Report: Tottenham Hotspur to open talks over new deal for defender
entry.content: Micky van de Ven Contract Talks Give Tottenham Fresh Hope After Roberto De Zerbi ResetTottenham Hotspur are preparing to open formal discussions with Micky van de Ven over a new long-term contract, in...

entry.title: Rory McIlroy re-ignites feud with Bryson DeChambeau and accuses him of holding The Open hostage
entry.content: Rory McIlroy spoke to the media after his third round at The Open Championship on Saturday and he was understandably asked about the Bryson DeChambeau fiasco on Friday. McIlroy and DeChambeau have never really seen eye-to-eye.

entry.title: How to track the 102nd Port Huron-to-Mackinac Island Sailboat Race
entry.content: You can keep up with your favorite teams as they race north online.

entry.title: Angel Reese Blasts Toronto Tempo Coach Sandy Brondello After Fans Claim Racism
entry.content: Angel Reese had a stellar performance during last night’s Atlanta Dream win over the Toronto Tempo. Scoring 23 points on The Angel Reese Blasts Toronto Tempo Coach Sandy Brondello After Fans Claim Racism first appeared on The Blast

entry.title: Rhode Island fugitive captured after more than 20 years on the run
entry.content: One of Rhode Island’s “longest-wanted” fugitives was finally arrested this week after 21 years on the run, according to the U.S.

entry.title: All the EVs that were discontinued or killed off in the U.S. this year
entry.content: Th Honda Prologue will no longer be sold in the U.S., joining a growing list of EV models to exit the market this year.

entry.title: Idaho pushed anti-trans laws for years. Then a teen died.
entry.content: The life and death of May Pollard, 16, provides a window into the tragic effect that legislation and political rhetoric have had on trans people in America.

entry.title: U.S. Emergency Oil Reserve Hits Lowest Levels Since 1983: Why It Matters
entry.content: Video: WSJ’s David Uberti explains how the Strategic Petroleum Reserve works to help blunt the impact of oil shocks such as the Iran War.

entry.title: Fans elated for Argentina and Spain to face off in World Cup final
entry.content: Millions of fans will watch as Argentina and Spain appear in the World Cup final. Spain looks to dethrone reigning champion Argentina, while Argentina hopes Lionel Messi leads them to glory again.

entry.title: License of Utah school where Paris Hilton alleged she was abused is revoked
entry.content: The license for another campus of the Provo Canyon School was revoked on Friday.

entry.title: White House doubles down on Trump's baseless election interference claims
entry.content: President Trump rehashed old talking points about election security, claiming without evidence that China interfered in the 2020 election during a primetime address. A day later, Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin announced an election security crackdown.

entry.title: Taylor Farms recalls iceberg lettuce after link to diarrhea-causing parasite
entry.content: Lettuce from supplier Taylor Farms was linked to a parasitic disease in an FDA investigation. The FDA advised consumers should avoid shredded iceberg lettuce distributed to Taco Bell locations in Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio and West Virginia. Taco Bell said it removed the lettuce, and Taylor Farms voluntarily recalled their impacted product.

entry.title: Canadian wildfire smoke blankets parts of U.S. as Texas floods turn deadly
entry.content: Smoke from Canadian wildfires still shrouds huge swathes of the U.S., from the Great Lakes region to the Mid-Atlantic. Down south, torrential rain brought flooding to Texas Hill Country. Plus, a look at the air quality forecast.

entry.title: Air quality warnings remain in place across US as wildfire smoke continues to swathe country
entry.content: Rain could alleviate conditions in mid-Atlantic and north-east, with World Cup final expected to go ahead on SundayWarnings of dangerous conditions are expected to remain in place on Saturday across swathes of the US, amid uncertainty about where the heavy wildfire smoke swirling from the Canadian province of Ontario and the US state of Minnesota will head next.Some parts of the US mid-Atlantic and north-east regions will continue to endure poor air quality until Saturday afternoon, where there is a high chance of thunderstorms, which could bring some reprieve from the poor air but come with other risks like flash flooding and high winds. Meanwhile, parts of the midwest and Great Lakes regions will continue to see dangerous air quality. Continue reading...

entry.title: Turkey Aims to Offload Russian Air-Defense System as It Seeks to Buy U.S. F-35s
entry.content: A proposed deal would entail transferring the S-400 to the U.A.E. in hopes that this would unblock Turkey’s purchase of advanced American jets.

entry.title: No contact between Roma-Kessie
entry.content: After Juventus’s snub with the unexpected signing of Celik, it seemed Roma were ready to return the favor to Frederic Massara by decisively entering the race for Franck Kessié. The Ivorian made a n...

entry.title: Argentina Vs. Spain Could Be “The Greatest World Cup Final Ever”, Senior NBCUniversal Telemundo Exec Says
entry.content: For the Spanish-language audience, Sunday’s showdown between Argentina and Spain could be “the greatest World Cup final ever,” as one senior NBCUniversal Telemundo exec sees it. Miguel Lorenzo, SVP of Sports Content for NBCUniversal Telemundo Enterprises, told Deadline the matchup has a number of elements capable of propelling ratings to one last record at the …

entry.title: Shedeur Sanders' half-effort OTA reps give Deshaun Watson a real shot at Browns QB1, Florio warns
entry.content: Browns QB battle takes a dramatic turn as an NFL insider questions a rookie's work ethic and a veteran's comeback puts the competition wide open

entry.title: FSU's recruiting class ranking after Ta'Shaen Poole's commitment
entry.content: The FSU Seminoles beat out several SEC schools for top 100 safety Ta'Shawn Poole, and he is now their highest-ranked commit.

entry.title: Rueben Bain Jr. already has tremendous value according to ESPN
entry.content: ESPN says Bucs rookie Rueben Bain Jr. is already worth a first-round pick, highlighting his production and pass-rush upside.

entry.title: LeBron James’ free agency frontrunner might actually be the 76ers
entry.content: LeBron the Sixers? It really might happen.

entry.title: The Washington Nationals put an historic beatdown on the A’s last night
entry.content: Led by Harry Ford and Andres Chaparro, the Nats put up an insane 23 runs last night in Sacramento
